narrative: # Residual Minimization And Scientific ML Reliability

confidence.rationale: The current directive requires a broad catalogue, application map, and five-toy-simulation suite; the mutable plan tables now add goals G4-G6 and milestones M-7-M-12 without changing the immutable directive text.

narrative: Broadened the plan of record from the previously validated single fixed-collocation package to a catalogue-oriented research program with explicit application mapping and simulation breadth criteria.
evidence: Current directive requires at least twenty attempted mechanisms, at least ten explicit candidate families, at least five theorem-quality cases or disproofs, and at least five toy simulations.
Prior M-3/M-5/M-6 remain validated as one catalogue entry rather than closure for the expanded run.

confidence.rationale: The next worker cycle will bootstrap the broad residual case catalogue and application risk map using prior validated collocation work as one entry and expanding across distinct mechanisms.

evidence: M-1 contains only five initial candidates; expanded directive requires at least twenty attempted mechanisms and ten explicit objective/function families.

confidence.rationale: Catalogue bootstrap artifacts are complete enough for auditor review, but M-7 is not marked validated by the worker.

narrative: Built M-7 broad residual-case catalogue, application risk map, and toy simulation triage plan. Catalogue integrates prior fixed-collocation theorem as CAT-01 and expands to 22 attempted mechanisms with explicit families, corrections, statuses, and next tests.

application_risk_map.md maps every catalogue entry to at least one application motif or guardrail role.
toy_simulation_plan.md names five initial simulations with expected CSV, figure, script, and test artifacts.

narrative: Validated M-7 broad residual-case catalogue and application risk map. Remaining concerns are scoped qualifications for future simulation/proof cycles, not blockers for catalogue bootstrap.
evidence: Catalogue row count verified as 22.
Application risk map includes CAT-01 through CAT-22 entry coverage.
Toy simulation plan includes CAT-01, CAT-02, CAT-06, CAT-09/CAT-10, and CAT-11 with expected CSV/figure/script/test artifacts.

confidence.rationale: The weak-norm high-frequency toy and negative-control baseline are generated and tested, but auditor validation is still pending.

evidence: pytest tests/test_hidden_mode_observability_toy.py passed as part of the 9-test tranche.
figure check data/hidden_mode_observability.png returned OK.

confidence.rationale: This tranche adds three toy simulations toward the five-simulation suite; it should not be marked validated until auditor review and remaining suite scope are complete.

confidence.rationale: Built the first M-9 admissibility/invariant tranche with a branch note, positivity/mass toy, focused test, and narrow catalogue/toy-summary updates; auditor validation still pending.

narrative: Built M-9 admissibility and invariant-constraint branch covering Burgers entropy nonselection, positivity/mass admissibility, and maximum-principle baseline, plus a positivity/mass toy with CSV, figure, and tests.
evidence: Burgers stationary upward jump satisfies Rankine-Hugoniot with speed zero but violates entropy selection for u_L<u_R.
Positivity/mass toy evaluates admissible interior, simplex boundary, and negative-concentration states.
Maximum-principle candidate is classified as a stability baseline under exact continuous residual and exact data.

confidence.rationale: M-9 success criteria are met: admissibility_invariant_branch.md analyzes Burgers entropy nonselection, positivity/mass admissibility, and a maximum-principle baseline; the Burgers note distinguishes weak residual satisfaction from entropy admissibility; the positivity/mass toy produces CSV/PNG artifacts and tests boundary plus negative states; validators show warnings only and no new M-9 blocking errors.

narrative: Validated the M-9 admissibility and invariant-constraint branch. Equality residuals can miss inequality/admissibility requirements, while the maximum-principle case is correctly recorded as a stability baseline under exact continuous residual and exact data.
evidence: Burgers stationary upward jump for u_L=-1,u_R=1 satisfies Rankine-Hugoniot with s=0 but is not the entropy solution; the branch note identifies entropy inequalities/Oleinik/Lax/vanishing-viscosity selection as certificates.

data/positivity_mass_toy.csv includes admissible interior and boundary simplex states plus negative-concentration states; all have aggregate_residual 0 while negative states have positive positivity_certificate.
Focused pytest command for the M-9 toy and existing cheap toy tests reported 18 passed.
Figure check for data/positivity_mass_toy.png returned 1332x1007 RGBA, 68916 bytes.
promise_check and org_check exited 0 with warnings only.

confidence.rationale: Built the M-8 weak/topology closure package with a branch report, localized-defect toy, focused tests, generated CSV/PNG, and narrow catalogue/toy-summary updates; auditor validation still pending.

narrative: Built M-8 weak-norm and topology-mismatch closure package with three candidates: high-frequency weak topology, localized mean-zero defect, and matched elliptic stability baseline. Added localized-defect CSV, figure, tests, and narrow catalogue/toy-summary updates.
evidence: CAT-06 high-frequency scaling remains J_s=(1+k^2)^(-s) with L2 error one.
Localized mean-zero defect keeps L2 norm one while H^{-1} objective decreases from 0.0094018 to 0.0003885 over tested epsilons, fitted log-log slope 1.839.
Matched elliptic residual is classified as a stability baseline by the H_0^1 <= H^{-1} energy estimate.

confidence.rationale: M-8 success criteria are met: weak_topology_branch.md analyzes three weak/topology candidates with explicit scaling laws; CAT-06 and WT-2 are direct weak-topology failures with fixed L2 scale and vanishing weak objective; WT-3 is correctly classified as a matched elliptic stability baseline. Focused tests and validators pass with warnings only.

narrative: Validated the M-8 weak-norm and topology-mismatch branch. Direct weak measurements of the solution can miss oscillatory and localized L2-scale defects, while a coercive elliptic residual measured in the correct dual norm remains a certificate rather than a failure.
evidence: weak_topology_branch.md lists WT-1/CAT-06, WT-2, and WT-3/CAT-04 with objective/norm, bad family or obstruction, scaling law, physical-error behavior, correction, and status.

Rerunning scripts/weak_norm_localized_defect.py regenerated CSV/PNG and reported fitted log-log slope 1.839; CSV rows keep L2 norm equal to one while H^{-1} objective decreases from 0.0094018 to 0.0003885.
Focused pytest command .sciml-venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_weak_norm_high_frequency_toy.py tests/test_weak_norm_localized_defect.py reported 6 passed.
data/weak_norm_localized_defect.png exists as a 1224x792 RGBA PNG and is referenced in toy_simulation_results.md.
promise_check and org_check exited 0 with warnings only; warnings are historical or pre-existing and not M-8 blockers.

confidence.rationale: Built the M-10 ODE reliability closure package with two new toy demonstrations and one stiffness stability baseline, but auditor validation remains pending.

narrative: Built M-10 ODE reliability branch covering hidden-mode observability, scalar stiffness stability baseline, Lyapunov/deployment-region mismatch, inverse-parameter non-identifiability, and the M-9 positivity/admissibility link. Added two ODE toy scripts with CSV, PNG, focused tests, and narrow catalogue/toy-summary updates.
evidence: Lyapunov toy: training residual at x=0 is zero for true and bad fields; deployment error from x0=1 grows to 7.253720815694038 at t=2; learned Lyapunov derivative at x=1 is +2 while true derivative is -2.

Parameter toy: zero trajectory gives zero state residual and zero data error for theta values -4,-2,-1,0,2,4; theta=4 has parameter error 5 relative to theta_star=-1; Fisher information is 0 for x0=0 and 0.08083089595423305 for x0=1 at theta_star.

Stiff scalar baseline: variation-of-constants estimate e(t)=int_0^t exp(-lambda(t-s)) r(s) ds records that matched continuous residual plus fixed initial data is stable, so stiffness alone is not promoted as an objective failure.
.sciml-venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_hidden_mode_observability_toy.py tests/test_lyapunov_stability_mismatch_toy.py tests/test_ode_parameter_nonidentifiability_toy.py reported 9 passed.
figure check returned OK for data/lyapunov_stability_mismatch.png and data/ode_parameter_nonidentifiability.png.

confidence.rationale: M-10 success criteria are met: ode_reliability_branch.md triages observability, stiffness, Lyapunov/deployment mismatch, inverse-parameter non-identifiability, and the M-9 positivity/admissibility link; two new bad families beyond CAT-11 are explicit and tested; the scalar stiff ODE is correctly classified as a matched continuous-residual stability baseline; focused tests and validators pass with warnings only.

narrative: Validated the M-10 ODE reliability branch. ODE residual objectives can fail by observing only a hidden-state projection, one training trajectory, or an unexcited parameter direction, while matched continuous residual control for the scalar stiff ODE is a stability baseline rather than an objective failure.
evidence: ode_reliability_branch.md lists CAT-11 hidden mode, ODE-SB stiff scalar baseline, CAT-14 Lyapunov/deployment-region mismatch, CAT-17 inverse parameter non-identifiability, and CAT-12/CAT-13 positivity/mass as a cross-branch link, with objective, failure or obstruction, residual behavior, physical error, certificate, and status.

Lyapunov CSV: training residual at x=0 is zero for true and bad fields; from x0=1 at t=2 true x=0.1353352832366127, bad x=7.38905609893065, deployment_abs_error=7.253720815694038, true Lyapunov derivative=-2 and bad derivative=2 at x=1.

Parameter CSV: zero-trajectory residual and state-data error are zero for theta values -4,-2,-1,0,2,4; theta=4 has parameter error 5 relative to theta_star=-1; Fisher information is 0 for x0=0 and 0.08083089595423305 for x0=1 at theta_star.

.sciml-venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_hidden_mode_observability_toy.py tests/test_lyapunov_stability_mismatch_toy.py tests/test_ode_parameter_nonidentifiability_toy.py reported 9 passed in 12.15s.
promise_check and org_check exited 0 with warnings only; warnings are historical or pre-existing and not M-10 blockers.
Figure check reports data/lyapunov_stability_mismatch.png and data/ode_parameter_nonidentifiability.png as 1206x756 RGBA PNG files.
